architecture.summary: The system augments a Transformer encoder-decoder (BART) with structured tensor-product representations (TPR). During encoding, the model learns to map input tokens into a compositional role-filler binding space where roles encode syntactic/semantic positions and fillers encode lexical content. This bound representation is injected into the decoder via a trained projection layer, enriching the Transformer's latent reasoning with explicit compositional structure before autoregressive decoding.

biblio.title: Enriching Transformers with Structured Tensor-Product Representations for Abstractive Summarization

claims.statement: The model achieves improvements over BART baseline on CNN/DailyMail abstractive summarization
Tensor-product representations enable the model to learn explicit role-filler bindings that capture syntactic and semantic structure
The structured representations improve interpretability by making compositional reasoning visible through role and filler assignments
The injected TPR structure helps the decoder generate more abstractive (rather than extractive) summaries
